Case Name: Pennington v. Mercy Regional Med. Ctr.
Defendant Action: Defendant issued a report with no malignancy
Expected Action (Court Standard): A thorough review should have been conducted

Case Name: Rivera v. New York City Health & Hosps. Corp.
Court: New York Supreme Court, Kings County
Defendant Action: No further action taken, treated symptomatically
Expected Action (Court Standard): Referral to vascular surgery should have been made

Case Name: Wilson v. Finkelstein
Chief Complaint: Stomach pain and bilateral flank pain
Defendant Action: Defendant recommended methotrexate without follow-up tests
Expected Action (Court Standard): Follow-up beta hCG test and ultrasound should have been ordered

Case Name: Gary Horndeski M.D. v. Cheryl Price
Chief Complaint: Patient presented for care and treatment to remove excess skin under each arm after breast cancer surgery
Defendant Action: Defendant proceeded with Bellasoma procedure
Expected Action (Court Standard): Surgery should not have been performed

Case Name: Estate of Crnjak v. Lake Hosp. Sys. Inc.
Chief Complaint: Possible urinary tract infection, left flank pain, and fever
Defendant Action: No contact made with patient after critical results
Expected Action (Court Standard): Patient should have been contacted and advised to return

Case Name: John R. Orphanos, M.D. v. Michael Rodgers
Court: Intermediate Court of Appeals of West Virginia
Defendant Action: Defendant proceeded with spinal surgery
Expected Action (Court Standard): Conservative management should have been continued

Case Name: Hargrove v. Medstar Washington Hospital Center
Defendant Action: MRI was deferred for safety reasons
Expected Action (Court Standard): MRI should have been performed immediately

Case Name: John Armour, Individually and as Personal Representative of the Estate of Judith Armour v. David Bader, M.D.
Defendant Action: Defendant sent patient home
Expected Action (Court Standard): Patient should have been sent to the emergency room

Case Name: Raouf Hanna, DDS and Hanna Dental Implants, LLLP v. Thomas Neil Turner
Chief Complaint: Desire for a permanent solution to dental issues
Defendant Action: Defendant performed a single panoramic x-ray
Expected Action (Court Standard): Full-mouth periapical x-rays and three-dimensional imaging should have been ordered

Case Name: Danhoff v. Fahim
Chief Complaint: Pain and fever one day after spinal surgery
Defendant Action: Dismissed pain and fever as normal surgical responses and proceeded with second planned surgery
Expected Action (Court Standard): Should have investigated post-operative fever and pain with imaging to rule out bowel injury, given the surgical approach passes near the sigmoid colon
